summary: [APU Health Monitor] Create query for APU Health Monitor in Engine Folder

output_fields.name: psn
output_fields.description: Unique PSN associated with each APU Serial Number

output_fields.name: ref_date
output_fields.description: Reference Date (latest date only for each APU)

transformations: Join rotables table to rotables_trend table on PSN field
Join rotables table to aircraft table on aircraft registration to obtain aircraft type
For each APU (identified by PSN), retain only the row with the latest ref_date value
Filter rotables_trend records to include only those with trend_type equal to CT5
acceptance_criteria: Query returns all APUs of type B737NG with their latest CT5ATP trend data
Each APU appears only once with its most recent reference date
All required fields (partno, serialno, psn, ac_registr, ref_date, trend_type, event_perfno_i, value, ac_typ) are present in output
Workorder information (event_perfno_i) is included when present, null when absent
VDS is created in Engine Folder
Query executes successfully against AMOS Acceptance database
Upon AMOS production go-live, data source can be switched to production without query logic changes
